Streamline pipeline: remove params.outdir overrides and consolidate shared processes

Task 1: Remove module-level params.outdir overrides from main.nf and all
affected module files (canoes, clamms, cnvkit, gatk-gcnv, evaluate,
feature-extraction, normalise, survivor, train, truvari). Also removed
unused outdir.mkdir() calls from main.nf and modules-evaluate.nf.

Task 2: Create modules/modules-common.nf with shared BGZIP_SORT_INDEX_VCF
and NORMALISE_CNV_QUALITY_SCORES processes parametrised by tool_annot/
caller_name and dir_suffix vals.

Task 3 (partial): Updated modules-canoes, modules-clamms, modules-xhmm,
modules-cnvkit, modules-indelible, and modules-gatk-gcnv to include from
modules-common.nf, remove duplicate process blocks, and pass the correct
caller/dir_suffix arguments in workflow calls.

fix: use PrepareForBuildDependsOn to ensure wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js is built before static web asset discovery

The Razor SDK's static web asset pipeline requires the wwwroot/ file to exist
before ResolveProjectStaticWebAssets and ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs
run. PrepareForBuildDependsOn runs our BuildNpmBundle target early enough (before
any build targets evaluate @(Content)) to satisfy this requirement.

Also use a relative Content item path ('wwwroot\cgscript-cm6.js') instead of an
absolute path so ResolveCurrentProjectStaticWebAssetsInputs correctly picks it up
(it filters @(Content) by identity starting with 'wwwroot/').

This restores the staticwebassets/ and build/*.props structure in the nupkg that
was present in v2.20.1 but lost when wwwroot/cgscript-cm6.js was gitignored.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: desktop mode with electron-mcp RPC/MCP on port 18101

Architecture:
  cicy-code --desktop
    → Go API server (port 18008)
    → electron (official binary) + electron-mcp JS (port 18101)
    → Electron window loads http://localhost:18008/?token=xxx

Key design: uses official Electron binary (npm i -g electron),
no code signing needed. electron-mcp provides RPC/MCP server
with 50+ tools (screenshot, automation, clipboard, CDP, etc).

Go backend:
  - desktop.go: ensureDesktop(), proxy /api/desktop/* → 18101
  - /api/desktop/status: desktop mode info + tool list
  - /api/desktop/rpc/*: proxy to electron-mcp RPC endpoints
  - /api/desktop/mcp: proxy to MCP SSE endpoint

npm bin:
  - --desktop flag: start API → wait health → launch electron
  - Discovery: global electron + global cicy → bundled desktop/
  - Auto-install electron and cicy if missing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: update desktop submodule to 7ef8161

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Discovery order: global 'cicy' CLI → bundled desktop/ → npx cicy
- Electron exit kills server, clean shutdown
- Add --desktop to local-deploy.md command table

fix: set TERM=xterm-256color on PTY child process

Root cause: exec.Command inherits parent process TERM which may be
empty/dumb when started via nohup, causing 'terminal does not support
clear' in browser terminal.

Fix: explicitly set TERM=xterm-256color in cmd.Env before pty.Start().

feat: add 12 progressive Terraform IaC training labs

Created LABS/ directory with comprehensive hands-on training labs:

- LAB-01: Terraform basics — init/plan/apply/destroy workflow with local_file
- LAB-02: Variables, outputs, locals — types, validation, tfvars, precedence
- LAB-03: Multiple resources — implicit/explicit dependencies, lifecycle meta-args
- LAB-04: Data sources — query existing infra, AWS AMI/AZ/VPC examples
- LAB-05: Modules — structure, local/registry/git sources, versioning
- LAB-06: State management — remote S3+DynamoDB backend, state commands
- LAB-07: Count & for_each — dynamic resources, index vs key addressing
- LAB-08: Provisioners & null_resource — local-exec, remote-exec, triggers
- LAB-09: Workspaces — multi-env management, workspace-specific configs
- LAB-10: Import — CLI import, declarative import block, -generate-config-out
- LAB-11: Testing — validate, fmt, tflint, checkov, tfsec, terratest
- LAB-12: CI/CD — GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Terraform Cloud, Atlantis

Each lab includes: learning objectives, step-by-step instructions,
working HCL/YAML code examples, expected outputs, verification checklists,
cleanup instructions, 3 challenges, and solution hints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- TOOLS.md: Required and recommended tooling (tflint, terraform-docs,

- RESOURCES.md: Curated books, courses, YouTube channels, community

- TROUBLESHOOTING.md: Common issues with root causes and step-by-step
  solutions including rollback strategies
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Contribution guidelines, development workflow,
  content standards, Terraform code standards, PR process
- LICENSE: MIT License (2024, ashad1990)
- .gitignore: Terraform-specific ignore rules
- .terraform-version: Pinned to 1.7.0 for tfenv/tfswitch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: adjust minimum Python version in broad charm compatibility tests (#2317)

A large number of our [broad charm compatibility
tests](https://github.com/canonical/operator/actions/workflows/published-charms-tests.yaml)
fail because the charm declares that it works with Python 3.8. That
conflicts with us pushing the latest ops 3.x into the dependencies.

Generally, if we've made a change to ops[...] that breaks charms, that
will be across Python versions, and we're more concerned with 3.10+
anyway (and in main, we're only supporting 3.10+). We could have some
compatibility tests that check the latest 2.x branch for charms that
expect 3.8, but that doesn't seem as useful.

This PR adjusts the dependency patching to also handle bumping the
Python version to 3.10. It [reduces the number of failures to around
6](https://github.com/tonyandrewmeyer/operator/actions/runs/23189775456),
some of which are only for static checks (which I haven't worked on in
this PR at all).

Since the changes are more complex now, they are moved out of the action
file (YAML+bash) and into a helper (Python) script. Primarily, we do the
work through tox, which I found to be the most consistent way to handle
things across packaging tools (particularly given that we are using
wheels now rather than git references).

The script also adds support for `tox.toml`, as some charms use that
rather than `tox.ini`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tonyandrewmeyer <826522+tonyandrewmeyer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Dima Tisnek <dimaqq@gmail.com>

Simplify share: clipboard + gh-slack only

Replace generic webhook support with gh-slack integration.
If gh-slack is installed and a channel is configured in
~/.config/gh-games/config.toml, players can post results
directly to Slack. Otherwise, clipboard copy is always available.

commit.message:
Add KeyboardAwareForm component and document in UX guidelines (#29) (#31)

* Initial plan

* feat: create KeyboardAwareForm component and apply globally to all form screens

- Add KeyboardAwareForm as a form-specific wrapper around KeyboardAwareScrollView
  with CTA-friendly default bottom offset (24px)
- Add comprehensive test suite for KeyboardAwareForm (7 tests)
- Migrate all form screens to use KeyboardAwareForm:
  - sign-in, sign-up (auth)
  - onboarding profile
  - profile tab, network tab, announcements tab
  - conversation screen
  - player-sports-section feature
- Fix messages list screen: replace raw ScrollView with KeyboardAwareScrollView



* refactor: rename DEFAULT_FORM_EXTRA_BOTTOM_OFFSET to DEFAULT_EXTRA_BOTTOM_OFFSET



* docs: add KeyboardAwareForm usage guidance to mobile UX guidelines



---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GigiProve <15339926+GigiProve@users.noreply.github.com>

Disable horizontal swipe-back gesture on root and auth stacks (#28)

* Initial plan

* fix: disable horizontal swipe-back gesture on root and auth stacks

Add gestureEnabled: false to root layout and auth layout Stack
screenOptions to prevent unintended horizontal screen swiping.
The onboarding flow retains its own gesture settings (controlled
per-step via canGoBack).

Co-authored-by: GigiProve <15339926+GigiProve@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(list): add allNamespaces flag to list_resources and get_events (#10) (#22)

Add `allNamespaces` boolean parameter to both `list_resources` and
`get_events` tools.

- allNamespaces=true lists resources/events across all namespaces by
  passing empty string as namespace to the API
- allNamespaces=true + explicit namespace returns a conflict tool error
- Tool descriptions updated to mention cross-namespace listing
- Unit tests added for both happy path and conflict error cases
- E2E tests added for allNamespaces listing and conflict validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(delete): add force flag to delete_resource (#11) (#21)

Add a force boolean parameter to delete_resource.

- force=true sets GracePeriodSeconds=0 for immediate deletion
- force=true + gracePeriodSeconds>0 returns a tool error (mutually exclusive)
- Elicitation prompt updated to warn about force delete side-effects
- Force delete still gated behind --allow-destructive

Unit tests cover: force path, conflict error, default behaviour, elicitation
E2E tests cover: force param accepted (dryRun), conflict validated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(apply): add validate parameter to apply_resource (#16)

Add a validate string parameter (strict/warn/ignore/none) that maps to
the Kubernetes fieldValidation query parameter on Create and Update calls.
Defaults to "strict" to catch schema errors before writing to the cluster.
Works with dryRun=true for a fully non-destructive validation check.

- shared resolveFieldValidation helper added to helpers.go
- 7 new unit tests in apply_test.go
- 5 new e2e subtests in e2e/apply_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(drain): add force and timeout parameters to drain_node (#15)

Add two new optional parameters to the drain_node MCP tool:

- force (boolean, default: false): when eviction fails, force-delete the
  pod directly instead of reporting an error. Intended for unmanaged pods
  (not owned by a ReplicaSet, Job, DaemonSet, or StatefulSet) that cannot
  be evicted via the eviction API. WARNING: these pods are permanently lost.

- timeout (float seconds, default: 0 = no timeout): set a deadline for the
  overall drain operation. The deadline is checked at the start of each pod
  iteration; if it has elapsed, the tool returns an error listing the pods
  that were not yet processed. Supports fractional seconds (e.g. 0.5).

Both parameters work alongside the existing ignoreDaemonSets,
deleteEmptyDirData, gracePeriodSeconds, and dryRun parameters. The tool
description is updated to mention the force risk (unmanaged pods lost).

Unit tests added (RED→GREEN):
- force=true deletes unmanaged pods via direct Delete when eviction fails
- force=false reports eviction error for unmanaged pods (existing behaviour)
- timeout fires mid-loop when first eviction blocks past the deadline
- timeout=0 imposes no deadline (no regression)
- force+timeout work together
- force+dryRun does not actually delete pods
- tool description mentions force risk

E2E smoke tests added (dryRun=true, parameter acceptance only):
- force param accepted
- timeout param accepted
- force+timeout together accepted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix inconsistent repo picker: use dropdown in both new session and multi-agent flows (#404)

## Bug
In 'New Session' the repository selector is a `<select>` dropdown, but
in 'New Multi-Agent' it was a list of clickable buttons. This
inconsistency is confusing.

## Fix
Changed the multi-agent repo picker (Step 1 in SessionSidebar.razor)
from a button list to a `<select>` dropdown matching CreateSessionForm:

- **Multiple repos**: `<select class="ns-repo-select">` dropdown with
placeholder option
- **Single repo**: `<div class="ns-repo-label">` label (auto-advances to
Step 2)
- **No repos**: Existing 'no repositories' message

Also added `OnMultiAgentRepoChanged` handler and auto-advance logic when
there's exactly 1 repo.

## Tests
Added `RepoPickerConsistencyTests.cs` (8 tests) verifying:
- Both forms use `<select>` with `ns-repo-select` class
- Multi-agent picker no longer uses button list
- Single-repo label behavior matches
- Auto-advance and placeholder option exist
- Handler exists for dropdown change event

All 2760 tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Shane Neuville <shneuvil@microsoft.com>

feat(rbac): add RBAC inspection tools (#17)

Add three read-only MCP tools for Kubernetes RBAC inspection:

- list_rbac_bindings: lists ClusterRoleBindings (no namespace) or
  RoleBindings (with namespace), with optional subject/subjectKind filters
- list_rbac_roles: lists ClusterRoles or Roles; returns detailed rules
  when a specific name is provided
- list_service_accounts: lists ServiceAccounts across all or a single
  namespace; detailed view exposes secret names but never token data

All three tools use the typed Kubernetes clientset (RbacV1/CoreV1),
are registered as read-only, and covered by 18 unit tests + e2e tests.

Build complete 2D top-down pirate game in Pygame

- Procedurally generated 8000x8000 ocean world (20 islands, 40+ rocks)
- Animated multi-layer ocean with waves, foam, day/night tinting
- Momentum-based player ship with wind/sail physics
- Enemy AI state machine: PATROL → CHASE → ATTACK → RETREAT → DEAD
- Broadside cannon combat with particle effects (explosion, smoke, splash)
- Weather system: CLEAR/CLOUDY/RAINY/STORM with rain, fog, lightning
- Treasure chests (bronze/silver/gold/legendary) worth 50-1000 gold
- Victory condition: collect 5000 gold
- Pirate-themed HUD: health, gold, compass, wind, day/night
- Animated menus: main, pause, game-over, victory
- Minimap with full-map mode (M key)
- All graphics procedurally drawn with pygame.draw (no external images)
- No numpy dependency; noise via math.sin/integer hash functions

Controls: WASD to sail, SPACE to fire, E to interact, M for map, ESC to pause

Create complete 2D top-down pirate game in Pygame

Implements a fully playable pirate game with:
- Procedural world generation (islands, rocks, treasures, enemy ships)
- Realistic sailing with wind physics and sail mechanics
- Naval combat with broadside cannons and enemy AI (patrol/chase/attack/retreat)
- Dynamic weather system (Clear → Cloudy → Rain → Storm)
- Day/night cycle with colour-graded lighting
- Particle effects (splash, explosion, smoke, sparkle, wake)
- Animated ocean renderer with multi-layer wave simulation
- Full HUD (health bar, gold counter, compass rose, wind arrow)
- Minimap with full-screen world map toggle (M)
- Main menu, pause, game-over and victory screens
- All sprites procedurally generated — no external image files
- Island dock repair mechanic (E near dock)
- Treasure chest pickup for victory condition (5,000 gold)

Files added:

  engine/{__init__,settings,utils,camera,game}.py
  world/{__init__,ocean,island,world_gen,minimap,weather}.py
  entities/{__init__,particles,cannonball,treasure,enemy_ship,player_ship}.py

commit.message:
Fix hive TUI: uppercase letter comparison for input validation

The letterSet was populated with uppercase runes from Game.Letters
but the TUI was comparing lowercase typed input against it, causing
every letter to be rejected as 'not in the puzzle'.

commit.message:
Fix hive case mismatch causing infinite loop on startup

uniqueLetters() was lowercasing letters while the dictionary stores
UPPERCASE words. FindValidWords() never matched anything, so NewGame()
looped forever trying to find a puzzle with >=20 valid words.

Fix: use ToUpper consistently throughout. NewGame() now completes in ~1ms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: 🎮 Terminal games as a GitHub CLI extension

commit.message:
Fix hive hang: pre-compute pangram candidates from dictionary

The previous approach randomly generated 7-letter combos and hoped
to find a matching pangram, which almost never succeeded — causing
an infinite loop. Now we scan the dictionary once for words with
exactly 7 unique letters and pick from that list directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: net_r_realized includes full trade outcome + engine-specific PTP params

Two bugs in simulate_trade_outcome():

1. net_r_realized = realized_r (PTP portion only)
   Should be total_r (PTP + remaining position result)
   Effect: losses showed -0.05R (commission only) instead of -1.05R
           wins showed 0.45R (PTP) instead of actual total outcome
   Fix: replace round(realized_r,3) -> round(total_r,3) on all exit paths

2. PTP params hardcoded to engine_params['judas_swing']
   Should use signal.engine_name to get correct engine's partial_tp_rr/vol
   Effect: ict_enigma (ptp_rr=1.75) and ict_venom (ptp_rr=1.0) both used
           judas_swing defaults instead of their optimizer-tuned values
   Fix: ep = settings.engine_params.get(signal.engine_name, {})

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: :wrench: switch from MySQL to MariaDB in test workflow

Replace the ad-hoc 'sudo systemctl start mysql' setup with a proper
mariadb:lts service container. This matches the production/local
MariaDB environment and fixes two failing e2e tests that 500'd due to
MySQL 8 vs MariaDB incompatibility in the withApiary JSON_OBJECT query.

commit.message:
feat: 3D grid visualization, interactive 2D topology, SSE streaming

3D Command View (/grid-3d):
- Interactive 3D topology with Three.js/React Three Fiber
- Station-specific models: generator (arc reactor core, cooling tower,
  smokestack with animated steam), solar farm (20 tilted PV panels,
  inverter), load substations (HV transformers, surge arresters)
- Catenary physics transmission lines (y = a·cosh(x/a))
- Animated power flow particles (sphere-based, speed ∝ power magnitude)
- Click-to-inspect: detailed telemetry/status overlay panel
- Hover effects with subtle scale animation
- Pulsing status glow rings on foundation pads
- Blinking aviation warning lights
- Auto-start simulation on page visit

2D Dashboard Upgrades:
- Draggable topology nodes in React Flow
- Click bus node → detailed right-side inspector panel
- Click transmission line → flow info modal
- Type-specific icons (⚡ generator, ☀️ solar, 🏭 load)
- Animated edges with flow dots and MW labels
- Attack/healing pulse animations on nodes

Data Streaming:
- New SSE hook (useSSEGridData) replaces 1s polling
- Persistent EventSource connection with auto-reconnect
- Shield status now published via EventBus
- Both 2D and 3D pages use SSE

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
